hints: Two operators do the heavy lifting: `//` gives you whole minutes (`total_seconds // 60`), and `%` gives you the leftover seconds (`total_seconds % 60`).
To pad single-digit seconds with a leading zero, use `:02d` inside your f-string: `f"{minutes}:{seconds:02d}"`.

Full solution:

minutes = total_seconds // 60
seconds = total_seconds % 60
return f"{minutes}:{seconds:02d}"
